medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient presents to the ED with chronic back pain, hx of microhematuria/recurrent UTIs, with ___ days of crampy, spasmodic R flank/RLQ pain. The pain is always present, but comes in waves. She's never had pain like this before. No nausea/vomiting, no dysuria/urinary symptoms, no change in bowels. She believes she may have had some intermittent chills. No numbness or tingling in her R leg. No hx of trauma. In the ED: - initial vitals: 98.0 84 118/55 24 100% RA - Labs were significant for: Hgb 10.2, WBC 3.9, lipase 148 - Imaging showed - Received IV cipro 400 mg /IV flagyl 500 mg x1, 5 IV morphine x3, ketorolac 30 mg x1, and 1 L NS - Vitals prior to transfer: 98.1 69 117/69 18 99% RA - Exam notable for moderate RLQ TTP without peritoneal signs, (+) R CVAT.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Microscopic hematuria, Cystitis (recurrent infections but mostly negative cultures), Hypertension, Hyperlipidemia, Chronic lower back pain s/p lumbar discectomies, TAH.
medical_history.family_history: No family hx of kidney stones, no family hx of IBD.

physical_examination.general_examination: ADMISSION PHYSICAL EXAM: VS: 97.7 123/72 59 18 98% RA. GEN: NAD, sitting in bed, about to eat dinner. ABD: + BS, non-tender on left side, mild suprapubic tenderness, and tenderness on R-side. No hepato-splenomegaly. +CVA tenderness.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: NEURO: CN II-XII grossly intact, motor function grossly normal.
